full_text:
The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H).

Dispase dissociation media included 14.55 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 300 µl Dispase + 150 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Filtered through 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 13.35 ml DMEM/F12+++, 1.5 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 150 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

RBC lysis with 5 ml for 4 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++ for both conditions.

The final pellet of dispase dissociated cells was resuspended in 3 ml of iPDC media and sequentially dissociated cells 4 ml of in DMEM/F12+++.

Cell counts:

Made 1 aliquot with 1 E6 cells from both dispase and sequentially dissociated cells.

The rest (43 E6 cells) from sequential dissociation were taken for enrichment with positive isolation (with anti-fibroblast beads).

Biobanking

Dispase dissociated cells: 2x 4.86 E6 cells

Cells were frozen in 1mL of STEM-CellBanker.

Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)

Performed positive isolation for the sequentially dissociated cells.

Positive isolation: Enriching the fibroblasts using anti-fibroblasts beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-050-601) and an LS Column (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-042-401) in a QuadroMACS separator (Miltenyi). Protocols by Miltenyi Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Centrifuged the cells at 1500 rpm for 5 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 336 µl of cold MACS buffer (calculation: 80 µl buffer per 10 E6 cells). Added 84 µl of anti-fibroblast beads to the cell suspension and incubated 30 min at RT shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 2 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm for 5 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

Placed an LS column and 30 µm filter in the magnetic MACS Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS buffer.

The column was removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly pushing the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed out.

Centrifuged 1500 rpm g for 5 min. Resuspended the column bound fraction in 2 ml iPDC media and the flowthrough fraction in 2 ml DMEM/F12+++ media. Counted the cells.

Took 1 E6 cells from the flowthrough fraction for flow cytometry.

Combining dispase dissociated cells and cells from the flowthrough fraction

Combined 5.99 E6 dispase dissociated cells and 5.99 E6 cells from the flowthrough fraction.

Took 4x 1 E6 aliquots for flow cytometry: 1x multicolor, 3x FMOs

Cell culture

From the combined cells, 40 uL droplets were seeded to test whether the different cell types can be maintained in culture over time. In flat-bottom, non-treated, 6-well plates (CytoOne, Cat CC7672-7506), 8 droplets were seeded per well as follows:

Cell density of combined fraction = 7.986 E6 cells/1666 uL = 4.99 E6 cells/mL (not taking into account the viability. However, viability was taking into account for the calculations shown below and how was done it’s explain)

Cell density in the droplets was 1000 live cells/uL, for a total of 40000 live cells/droplet. Ratio of dispase fraction to non-immune stroma-enriched fraction was 1:1.

Cells will be harvested at days 3, 7, and 9, using the dispase dissociation protocol.

To prepare the droplets, the following ratios were used:

The omentum gel in which the cells were embedded was from the Batch 3 received on 06.02.2025.

Calculations were done as follow:

1000 live cells/uL * 40 uL = 40000 cells/droplet * 8 droplets/well * 7 wells (1 extra) =

2.24 E6 cells are needed in total

8 droplets/ well * 7 wells * 40 uL/ droplet = 2 240 uL are needed in total

However, since the omentum gel starts polymerizing as soon as the fibrinogen is added to the mixture, it is better to make four (4) separate tubes each one of 560 uL (2240 uL/4) and not everything at once in a single tube.

We want to keep a cell density of 1 000 live cells/uL, so the total number of cells per tube that will be needed is:

1 000 cells/uL * 560 uL = 0.56 E6 cells/tube

0.56 E6 cells / (2.87 E6 cells/mL***) = 0.195 mL/tube = 195.12 uL (from the combined cell suspension)

*** This was the LIVE cell density in the combined cell suspension (taking into account the mean viability: (viability of dispase fraction + viability of fibroblasts) / 2)

The amount of the remaining components per tube will be:

ORG full medium: (560 uL – 333.5 uL) = 226.5 uL of supplemented media (iPDC media + cytokines)

Omentum gel: 123.8

Aprotinin: 1.68

Thrombin: 1.68

Fibrinogen: 11.2

Total (ome-gel+apro+thro+fib) = 138.4 uL

Total (+cells) = 333.5 uL

After plating, droplets were allowed to solidify for 60 min in the incubator, and when the time was up, 3.5 mL of complete media (iPDC media + cytokines) were added into each well.

Flow cytometry

The conditions to stain were:

DCM FMO

FAP FMO

a-SMA FMO

dispase dissociated cells, MC

anti-fibroblast, column bound, MC

combined cells, MC

The aliquots were stained with the following panel.Washed cells once with 900 µl FACS buffer (centrifuge 6min 400 g at +4°C).

The antibodies were diluted in Brilliant stain buffer (#563794 BD Horizon), 50 µl of the antibody mix was added to each sample, and the samples were incubated for 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

For the FMO samples, antibody mixes were prepared separately

After incubation, washed once with 900 µl FACS buffer.

Added 200 µl IC Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) to fix the cells and incubated for 40 min at +4°C shielded from light.

After incubation, washed once with 900 µl FACS buffer.

Resuspended in 900 µl FACS buffer for overnight storage.

Next morning, centrifuged and resuspended the cells in 350 µl FACS buffer.

Pipeted the cell suspension through a 5 ml Falcon polystyrene tube with a cell-strainer cap (blue).

Note: a-SMA was mistakenly added as this antibody it’s meant for intracellular staining so it should be used after fixation and permeabilization. Despite this, we decided to proceed as this shouldn’t cause major problems. However, when the cells were harvested in d3, d6, and d9, a-SMA was not added.

Preparation of compensation controls

Used BD CompBeads Plus (7.5 µm) compensation beads.

Added one drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG, #51-9006274) and negative (CompBeads Negative Control #51-9006227) compensation beads to 5ml Falcon polystyrene round bottom tubes (white cap).

For stained compensation controls, 2 µl of each antibody was used.

the same antibodies except CD16-BV510 instead of BV510-DCM.

for aSMA, 2.5 µl instead of 2 µl

Additionally, made compensation controls of CD8 and CD3 with 1 µl but saw in Fortessa that the one with 2 µl was slightly brighter and therefore used them.

Incubated for 30 min at +4°C shielded from light and resuspended to 350 µl FACS buffer.
